title: High Molecular Weight Genomic DNA Extraction from Gram-negative Bacteria for Long Reads Sequencing
author: Baptiste Baptiste Mayjonade
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.r9id94e

steps.title: Inoculate a single bacterial colony into...
steps.instruction: Inoculate a single bacterial colony into 10 mL of 10% TSB (i.e. 3g/l) (Fisherbrand™, Tryptone Caseine Soy Broth Product Code.12186620). Cultivate the bacteria for 48h at 28°C under constant agitation (120 rpm). Note: Culture medium and growth time can impact the purity of the gDNA (especially for bacterial species secreting extracellular polysaccharides like Xanthomonas ssp. ). See "Assessment of various parameters.xlsx" file attached in the abstract section.

steps.title: Heat the lysis buffer for >30...
steps.instruction: Heat the lysis buffer for >30 minutes at 65°C The composition of the lysis buffer is given in the Guidelines.

steps.title: Centrifuge 1
steps.instruction: Centrifuge 1.8 mL of bacterial culture in a 2 mL tube at 10 000g for 5 minutes

steps.title: Discard the supernatant by pouring off...
steps.instruction: Discard the supernatant by pouring off the liquid removing all growth media.

steps.title: Set a P1000 pipette on 900...
steps.instruction: Set a P1000 pipette on 900 µl and resuspend the bacterial pellet into 900 µl of lysis buffer (preheated at 65°C) by pipetting up and down vigorously 10 times (with an uncut P1000 tip). This step is critical for the yield and it is very important to pipette the complete liquid up and down vigorously so all the liquid enters the pipette tip during mixing. Apparition of foam is good.

steps.title: Incubate at 65°C in a dry...
steps.instruction: Incubate at 65°C in a dry bath for 20 minutes (invert the tube vigorously 10 times after 10 minutes). A temperature below 60°C can significantly reduce the yield due to incomplete lysis. See "Assessment of various parameters.xlsx" file attached in the abstract section. Delete Edit

steps.title: Spin down the tube for 1 second
steps.instruction: Spin down the tube for 1 second

steps.title: Add 4 µl of RNAse A...
steps.instruction: Add 4 µl of RNAse A 100 mg/ml (QIAGEN, Cat No./ID: 19101)

steps.title: Mix by inverting the tube 10...
steps.instruction: Mix by inverting the tube 10 times and spin down for 1 second

steps.title: Incubate at room temperature (18-23°C) for 10 minutes
steps.instruction: Incubate at room temperature (18-23°C) for 10 minutes

steps.title: Add 300 µl of 5M Potassium...
steps.instruction: Add 300 µl of 5M Potassium Acetate solution (or 1/3 of the lysis buffer volume)

steps.title: Mix by pipetting vigorously and completely...
steps.instruction: Mix by pipetting vigorously and completely 10 times with a P1000 set on 1000 µl and using a P1000 tip cut at 0.5 cm from the tip end (to minimize DNA shearing). This step is very important to precipitate contaminants and therefore will impact the purity of the final gDNA solution. The solution gets very viscous after Potassium Acetate addition and mixing by inverting the tube is not efficient enough.

steps.title: Centrifuge the tube at 10 000g...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the tube at 10 000g for 10 minutes at room temperature

steps.title: Transfer the upper phase into a...
steps.instruction: Transfer the upper phase into a new 2 mL tube without disturbing the pellet (typically 800µl)

steps.title: Add the same volume (800 µl)...
steps.instruction: Add the same volume (800 µl) of beads solution previously prepared (make sure beads are at room temperature for >30 minutes and well homogenized via vortexing for approximately 30 seconds)

steps.title: Incubate on a rotator at 10...
steps.instruction: Incubate on a rotator at 10 rpm for at least 15 minutes at room temperature

steps.title: Spin down the tube for 1 second
steps.instruction: Spin down the tube for 1 second

steps.title: Place the tube on a magnetic...
steps.instruction: Place the tube on a magnetic rack and wait for 3 minutes (until beads are stuck to the wall of the tube and solution becomes clear)

steps.title: Discard the supernatant by pipetting without taking out any beads
steps.instruction: Discard the supernatant by pipetting without taking out any beads

steps.title: Add 1 mL of fresh 70% ethanol
steps.instruction: Add 1 mL of fresh 70% ethanol

steps.title: Close the tube and resuspend the...
steps.instruction: Close the tube and resuspend the beads by inverting the tube 20 times. It is very important that the beads are resuspended in ethanol to ensure an efficient wash. If beads are still stuck to the wall of the tube you can flick the tube to resuspend them

steps.title: Spin down the tube for 1 second
steps.instruction: Spin down the tube for 1 second

steps.title: Put the tube back on the magnetic rack
steps.instruction: Put the tube back on the magnetic rack. At this step, beads move immediately to the wall of the tube

steps.title: Remove the supernatant without disturbing the beads
steps.instruction: Remove the supernatant without disturbing the beads

steps.title: Repeat the steps 20 to 24...
steps.instruction: Repeat the steps 20 to 24 to wash the beads one more time.

steps.title: Spin down the tube to bring...
steps.instruction: Spin down the tube to bring all the droplets to the bottom of the tube

steps.title: Put the tube back on the...
steps.instruction: Put the tube back on the magnetic rack and remove all the ethanol with a P200 or P10 pipette. Let beads air drying for a maximum of 30 seconds or else it can affect the DNA integrity and reduce the yield.

steps.title: Add 100 µl of Elution Buffer (QIAGEN, Cat No./ID
steps.instruction: Add 100 µl of Elution Buffer (QIAGEN, Cat No./ID: 19086) The composition of Buffer EB is: 10 mM Tris-Cl, pH 8.5.

steps.title: Resuspend the beads by flicking the...
steps.instruction: Resuspend the beads by flicking the tube (make sure they are not aggregated anymore).

steps.title: Incubate the tube for 15 minutes at 37°C
steps.instruction: Incubate the tube for 15 minutes at 37°C.

steps.title: Flick the tube again
steps.instruction: Flick the tube again

steps.title: Spin down the tube for 1 second
steps.instruction: Spin down the tube for 1 second

steps.title: Place the tube on a magnetic...
steps.instruction: Place the tube on a magnetic rack and wait for the solution to become clear. This can be time consuming when the gDNA solution is very concentrated (>200ng/µl). You can add additional elution buffer or just wait for hours!

steps.title: Transfer the solution into a new tube
steps.instruction: Transfer the solution into a new tube.

steps.title: If this ratio is >2 it...
steps.instruction: If this ratio is >2 it is likely that the sample is contaminated by RNA. RNA contamination also leads to a shift between Qubit and Nanodrop concentrations. To get rid of RNA, you can follow the additional purification n°1 below (Additional purification

steps.title: RNA removal starting from step 36)
steps.instruction: RNA removal starting from step 36). A260/A230 ratio should be 2 – 2.

steps.title: If this ratio is <2 you...
steps.instruction: If this ratio is <2 you can follow the purification below (Additional purification

steps.title: A260/A230 improvement starting from
steps.instruction: A260/A230 improvement starting from

steps.title: Qubit measurement is the most accurate...
steps.instruction: Qubit measurement is the most accurate as it is specific for double stranded DNA. Overestimation of the DNA concentration by the Nanodrop means that there are other compounds which absorb at 260 nm and therefore the gDNA is not pure.

steps.title: Add 1µl of RNase A 100...
steps.instruction: Add 1µl of RNase A 100 mg/ml for 100µl of gDNA solution

steps.title: Check the DNA integrity by running...
steps.instruction: Check the DNA integrity by running the sample on PFGE or capillary electrophoresis. The sample should range from 10-20kb to 100-150kb.

steps.title: Mix by inverting the tube 10 times
steps.instruction: Mix by inverting the tube 10 times

steps.title: Incubate for 10 minutes at room temperature (18-23°C)
steps.instruction: Incubate for 10 minutes at room temperature (18-23°C)

steps.title: Add 1 volume of beads solution...
steps.instruction: Add 1 volume of beads solution prepared (make sure beads are at room temperature for >30 minutes and well homogenized via vortexing for approximately 30 seconds)

steps.title: Follow the steps 16 to 34
steps.instruction: Follow the steps 16 to 34.

steps.title: Check the DNA purity and integrity...
steps.instruction: Check the DNA purity and integrity as in the steps 35-36.

steps.title: Some contaminants can be removed with...
steps.instruction: Some contaminants can be removed with this purification and lead to an increase of the A260/A230 ratio. But some contaminants coprecipitate with the DNA and therefore it is impossible to get rid of them with this purification.

steps.title: Add 1 volume of beads solution...
steps.instruction: Add 1 volume of beads solution previously prepared (make sure beads are at room temperature for >30 minutes and well homogenized via vortexing for approximately 30 seconds)

steps.title: Follow the step 16 to 34
steps.instruction: Follow the step 16 to 34.

steps.title: Check the DNA purity and integrity...
steps.instruction: Check the DNA purity and integrity as in the steps 35-36.
